discussionPosts.cooked: What was saved? Oh, only the entire imperial census Love that Thank you for playing! I hope you had fun

Quis Custodiet Ipsos Custodes? We Do Now. Friends, Romans, Kubernetes operators — lend me your ears. I am proud to announce that the Empire is safe once more! The census rolls of Gallia are no longer bleeding into the wrong hands, the gate stands firm against those who would abuse the power of unchecked privilege, and the Praetorian Guard has finally been roused from his embarrassingly long nap. What was saved? Oh, only the entire imperial census — the names, families, and registered auxiliaries of 2,841 citizens of Gallia, whose private registrations were being read by an uninvited speculator every 30 seconds like it was the morning gazette. The Empire’s admission laws now mean what they say. The Archive is sealed. The Guard is watching. And any intruder who fancies themselves clever enough to slip through the gates will find the doors very firmly shut — no matter what they are wearing or what they call their luggage. SPQR. The Republic endures.
